meaning_english: A state of temporary disuse or suspension. Synonyms: Suspension, Dormancy, Latency, Intermission.
A flattering or pleasing statement used to persuade. Synonyms: Flattery, Cajolery, Wheedling, Coaxing.
Rude in a mean-spirited and surly way. Synonyms: Ill-mannered, Boorish, Oafish, Sullen.
Remove the moisture from something; cause to become dry. Synonyms: Dehydrate, Parch, Shrivel, Wither.
Relating to or dependent on charity. Synonyms: Charitable, Philanthropic, Benevolent, Alms-giving.
Instigate or stir up (an undesirable or violent sentiment). Synonyms: Instigate, Incite, Provoke, Agitate.
Deny or contradict (a fact or statement). Synonyms: Contradict, Dispute, Repudiate, Deny.
Denoting a period of time in the past that was idyllically happy and peaceful. Synonyms: Serene, Tranquil, Placid, Peaceful.
An extremely confused, complicated, or embarrassing situation. Synonyms: Complication, Entanglement, Mess, Quandary.
Naive, simplistic, and superficial; or dry and uninteresting. Synonyms: Dull, Insipid, Childish, Vapid.
The sound of a bell, especially when rung solemnly for a death or funeral. Synonyms: Toll, Chime, Dirge, Omen.
Looking or sounding sad and dismal. Synonyms: Mournful, Gloomy, Melancholy, Doleful.
Given to begging. Synonyms: Beggar, Pauper, Friar, Petitioner.
The lowest point in the fortunes of a person or organization. Synonyms: Lowest point, Bottom, Rock-bottom.
Render obscure, unclear, or unintelligible. Synonyms: Confuse, Blur, Muddle, Cloud.
Make (a disease or its symptoms) less severe or unpleasant without removing the cause. Synonyms: Alleviate, Mitigate, Soothe, Ease.
In a state or period of inactivity or dormancy. Synonyms: Inactive, Latent, Idle, Passive.
Formidable, especially as an opponent. Synonyms: Formidable, Fearsome, Daunting, Mighty.
Health-giving; healthy. Synonyms: Wholesome, Beneficial, Salutary, Healthful.
Evasion of straightforward action or clear-cut statement. Synonyms: Equivocation, Evasion, Shuffling, Prevarication.
Offense or annoyance. Synonyms: Resentment, Pique, Grudge, Indignation.
Bitter and abusive. Synonyms: Abusive, Insulting, Scathing, Slanderous.
A large number of items in no order; a confused mass. Synonyms: Jumble, Mess, Confusion, Clutter.
Having or showing a dislike of or prejudice against people from other countries. Synonyms: Ethnocentric, Intolerant, Narrow-minded.
A wooden crosspiece that is fastened over the necks of two animals and attached to the plow or cart. Synonyms: Bondage, Oppression, Burden, Tie.
The time at which something is most powerful or successful. Synonyms: Peak, Pinnacle, Summit, Apex.
Solemnly renounce (a belief, cause, or claim). Synonyms: Renounce, Relinquish, Reject, Forgo.
So lacking in originality as to be obvious and boring. Synonyms: Trite, Hackneyed, Commonplace, Cliche.
Given to sudden and unaccountable changes of mood or behavior. Synonyms: Fickle, Unpredictable, Mercurial, Volatile.
Humble submission and respect. Synonyms: Respect, Homage, Reverence, Obedience.
Cause (someone) to feel drained of energy or vitality. Synonyms: Exhaust, Fatigue, Weaken, Devitalize.
Silly and pointless. Synonyms: Inane, Foolish, Asinine, Vacuous.
Excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters. Synonyms: Loquacious, Voluble, Chatty, Verbose.
A lengthy and aggressive speech. Synonyms: Tirade, Diatribe, Rant, Polemic.
Just begun and so not fully formed or developed. Synonyms: Nascent, Embryonic, Rudimentary, Formless.
Place or deal with close together for contrasting effect. Synonyms: Contrast, Collate, Compare, Side-by-side.
Displaying or having a disinclination for physical exertion or effort. Synonyms: Lethargic, Sluggish, Listless, Inert.
Able to be hammered or pressed permanently out of shape without breaking. Synonyms: Pliable, Ductile, Tractable, Adaptable.
A person who is new to a subject, skill, or belief. Synonyms: Beginner, Novice, Tyro, Greenhorn.
Involving a great deal of effort, trouble, or difficulty. Synonyms: Burdensome, Arduous, Taxing, Heavy.
Spreading widely throughout an area or a group of people. Synonyms: Ubiquitous, Prevalent, Widespread, Rife.
Exceedingly idealistic; unrealistic and impractical. Synonyms: Idealistic, Romantic, Visionary, Impractical.
Not revealing one's thoughts or feelings readily. Synonyms: Reserved, Taciturn, Introverted, Withdrawn.
Optimistic or positive, especially in an apparently bad or difficult situation. Synonyms: Optimistic, Hopeful, Cheerful, Buoyant.
Reserved or uncommunicative in speech; saying little. Synonyms: Reticent, Quiet, Silent, Laconic.
Present, appearing, or found everywhere. Synonyms: Omnipresent, Pervasive, Universal, Everywhere.
Alternate or waver between different opinions or actions; be indecisive. Synonyms: Waver, Dither, Hesitate, Teeter.
Feeling or showing caution about possible dangers or problems. Synonyms: Cautious, Circumspect, Vigilant, Alert.
Relating to institutionalized hospitality, especially for guests from other lands. Synonyms: Hospitable, Friendly, Welcoming.
A person who is fanatical and uncompromising in pursuit of their religious, political, or other ideals. Synonyms: Fanatic, Radical, Enthusiast, Extremist.
